objective.subject: English
objective.statement: Infer a character's feelings, thoughts or motives from actions and words and support the inference with relevant evidence.
misconceptions: Treats an inference as a guess that does not need a text clue.
Names a feeling that is possible in real life but not supported by this text.
Selects a whole sentence or vivid detail instead of the most relevant evidence.
States evidence and inference separately without explaining the link.
objective.parent_explanation: Can work out what a character may feel, think or want by using actions and words as clues.
objective.teacher_evidence: Stores the inference, selected text span and explanation separately, allowing teachers to distinguish comprehension, evidence selection and written-language demand.

activities.title: I can use a character's actions and words as clues, then explain what they suggest.
activities.prompt: Follow the path: notice an action or word, infer what it suggests, then point back to the clue.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Sort each detail: does the text say it directly, or does the detail suggest it?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Separate retrieval from inference before introducing justification.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Some answers are stated. Other answers are worked out from clues.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Detail cards move into SAY and SUGGEST columns with persistent text references.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Follow the path: notice an action or word, infer what it suggests, then point back to the clue.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Establish inference as a reversible clue-to-idea reasoning chain.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The clue leads to an idea, and the idea must still fit the clue.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Three fixed cards read CLUE, INFERENCE and BECAUSE, joined by directional arrows.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Tariq folded the note into a tiny square and pushed it deep into his pocket. His action suggests he wants to hide the note.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model a motive inference from precise action evidence while avoiding unsupported emotion labels.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Pushing the note deep into his pocket is the clue. It suggests a hiding motive because he puts it out of sight.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Only the action phrase is highlighted; a motive card connects with a spoken because explanation.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Choose a plausible inference, highlight the smallest useful clue, and complete the because link.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Coordinate inference, evidence precision and explanation with scaffolded choices.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Choose an idea that fits, then highlight only the words that help prove it.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: An extract remains visible above inference cards, a keyboard highlighter and a because sentence frame.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Which answer fits the clue without claiming more than the text shows?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Detect unsupported certainty and plausible-but-textless guessing.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Strong readers say what the clue supports, not everything that might be possible.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Three inference cards sit on evidence blocks; unsupported claims tip away.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach your companion how two inferences can both be plausible if each one uses a different clue.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Gather oral reasoning evidence and prevent one-word-answer rigidity.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Show me the clue for each idea and explain why both could fit.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Two inference paths branch from separate highlighted phrases in the same extract.

vocabulary.child_explanation: An idea worked out from clues the writer gives.
vocabulary.audio_script: An inference is a sensible idea made from clues in the text.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A clue card joining an action to a thought bubble.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The exact words or details that support an idea.
vocabulary.audio_script: Evidence is the part of the text that makes your inference believable.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A short highlighted phrase beneath a magnifying glass.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The reason why a character acts.
vocabulary.audio_script: A motive is what a character wants or the reason for an action.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: An action arrow pointing back to a hidden reason card.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Believable because it fits the clues.
vocabulary.audio_script: A plausible inference fits what the text shows, even if more than one answer is possible.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Several idea cards, with supported cards resting on clue blocks.

manipulative.purpose: Make the relationship between exact text evidence, a plausible inference and the explanation explicit.
manipulative.interaction_notes: Learners select text by keyboard or pointer, attach an inference card and complete an oral, typed or choice-based because link.
manipulative.accessibility_notes: Extracts expose line and sentence navigation; Shift+Arrow selects text, Enter confirms, and an alternative phrase-list mode removes fine text selection. Read-aloud highlights one sentence without auto-scrolling, all inference cards are screen-reader labelled, and speech input is optional rather than required.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use static cream text panels, one non-flashing outline highlight and no animated character faces or ambient movement.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Replace moving clue paths and balance effects with persistent numbered arrows and supported/unsupported labels.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Offer human-paced read-aloud by sentence, replay the question separately and pause after dialogue punctuation without adding interpretive emotion.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use 40-100 word extracts, adjustable line spacing, sentence masking, vocabulary glosses that do not reveal the inference and a phrase-list selection alternative.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Keep the source text visible, present one decision at a time and use a fixed CLUE-INFERENCE-BECAUSE progress strip.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Remove timers, retain selected evidence while the learner composes, and allow oral, typed or structured-choice explanations.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Describe alternatives as more or less supported, preserve plausible thinking during repair and model that two answers can fit when both have evidence.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach feeling, action and motive vocabulary with short scenarios; permit oral rehearsal and accept semantically equivalent evidence explanations.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Infer a feeling from a precise action, body-language or speech clue.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: 50-90 word age-appropriate extract + feeling prompt + one minimal supporting phrase + one broader acceptable phrase
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Avoid treating one behaviour as proof of a diagnosis or fixed personality; context must make the inference plausible.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Distinguish a text-supported thought or motive from merely possible alternatives.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: short extract + thought/motive question + one best-supported answer + two plausible unsupported distractors + one contradiction
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Distractors must differ in evidence support, not rely on obscure vocabulary.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Explain how selected evidence supports an inference.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: extract + accepted inference set + exact clue spans + because-link stems + semantic scoring notes
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Accept varied wording when the relationship is clear; never require one exact emotion synonym.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Revise an inference that is too certain, too broad or based on irrelevant detail.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: extract + flawed response + select flaw + rewrite using modal language or a narrower claim
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Model phrases such as might, may and suggests without making all responses formulaic.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Maintain clue selection across stories, letters, diary entries and short playscripts.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: spaced review queue + controlled 40-100 word extract + one inference + one evidence selection
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Control decoding and background-knowledge demand so inference remains the assessed construct.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Treats an inference as a guess that does not need a text clue.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner chooses an idea but leaves evidence blank or points to no relevant text.
repair_sequence: Return to the exact question and identify whether it asks about feeling, thought or motive.
Offer two short action clues from the extract.
Select the clue that fits and complete: I think this because...
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner chooses a plausible inference and independently highlights a supporting phrase in a new extract.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Names a feeling that is possible in real life but not supported by this text.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The answer is not contradicted by the text but cannot be linked to any supplied action or word.
repair_sequence: Compare the chosen feeling with one text-supported alternative.
Test each answer by asking which exact clue would prove it.
Keep the answer that rests on evidence and reject the textless possibility.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner distinguishes best-supported from merely possible choices and explains the difference.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Selects a whole sentence or vivid detail instead of the most relevant evidence.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Highlight includes unrelated detail or misses the phrase carrying the inference.
repair_sequence: Restate the inference as a focused question.
Remove words one phrase at a time while checking whether the evidence still works.
Keep the smallest useful clue and explain its relevance.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner selects a concise relevant span without losing necessary context.

misconception_repairs.misconception: States evidence and inference separately without explaining the link.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Response contains a plausible idea and quote but no account of what the quote shows.
repair_sequence: Place the clue and inference on separate cards.
Insert because or this suggests between them.
Say what the action, body language or words reveal.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner gives a complete clue-inference explanation in a fresh item, orally or in writing.

feedback.companion_prompt: Teach your companion how two inferences can both be plausible if each one uses a different clue.
feedback.parent_summary: Can work out what a character may feel, think or want by using actions and words as clues.
feedback.teacher_evidence: Stores the inference, selected text span and explanation separately, allowing teachers to distinguish comprehension, evidence selection and written-language demand.
next_actions: Route unsupported guesses to SAY versus SUGGEST sorting.
Route broad highlighting to smallest-useful-clue repair.
Introduce two-clue and changing-feeling passages after single-clue reasoning is secure.
Schedule mixed narrative and dialogue inference at 1, 3, 7, 14 and 30 days.

body.prompt: Highlight the words that best suggest Ava is uncertain.
body.extract: Ava reached for the door handle, stopped, and read the sign for a third time.

expected_answer.value: stopped, and read the sign for a third time
accepted_spans: read the sign for a third time

hints: Look for an action that shows she is not ready to continue.
Choose the smallest words that support uncertain.
questions.explanation: Stopping and checking the sign again suggest Ava is uncertain about going through the door.

body.prompt: Why does Tariq push the note deep into his pocket?
body.extract: Tariq folded the note into a tiny square. When Rosa looked across, he pushed it deep into his pocket and began talking about lunch.
choices: He wants to hide the note from Rosa
He has forgotten that the note exists
He wants Rosa to read the note immediately

expected_answer.value: He wants to hide the note from Rosa

hints: Notice what he does when Rosa looks across.
Which choice explains both hiding the note and changing the subject?
questions.explanation: Pushing the note deep into his pocket when Rosa looks and changing the subject suggest that Tariq wants to hide it from her.

body.prompt: Complete the explanation: Nia may feel relieved because...
body.extract: The missing key flashed beneath the bench. Nia let out a long breath and her shoulders dropped.

expected_answer.value: she finds the missing key, then lets out a long breath and her shoulders drop

hints: Use what happens before and after she sees the key.
Explain what her relaxed body language suggests.
questions.explanation: Finding the key solves the problem, and Nia's long breath and dropped shoulders suggest tension has left her.

body.prompt: Improve this answer so it says only what the clue supports: 'Milo is always dishonest.'
body.extract: Milo slid the cracked cup behind the plant before Dad entered the room.

expected_answer.value: Milo may be trying to hide that he cracked the cup because he slides it behind the plant.

hints: The extract shows one action, not what Milo is always like.
Use may or might and refer to the cup.
questions.explanation: The action supports a possible hiding motive in this moment, but it does not prove that Milo is always dishonest.

reward_payload.explanation: Can work out what a character may feel, think or want by using actions and words as clues.
reward_payload.companion_prompt: Teach your companion how two inferences can both be plausible if each one uses a different clue.
